name: Carne Mechasa
recipeIngredient: 1 Green pepper; sliced into thin slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a deep horizontal slit in the steak to form a pocket and set aside.
2. Cook the green pepper in salted water for five minutes, drain, and set aside.
3. Stuff the steak with the bacon, chiles, and green pepper. Sew the pocket shut.
4. In a heavy skillet, brown the meat in the reserved bacon drippings, then transfer to a 12x8" baking dish.
5. Combine tomato sauce, sugar, vinegar, salt, and bay leaf in the skillet. Heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves.
6. Pour half of this sauce over the meat, reserving the rest. Cover the baking dish and bake for 1 1/2 hours, basting occasionally with the pan juices.
7. When the meat is tender, remove from the oven and transfer it to a heated platter.
8. Skim any fat from the pan juices, then add water to the juices to make 2 1/4 cups liquid.
9. Put the liquid in the skillet. Blend the reserved sauce with the flour, and stir into the liquid.
10. Cook and stir until thick and bubbly. Spoon the sauce over the meat and serve.
